reasoning: I will vote for Biswajit Das and TMC because Mamata has promised no NRC in Bengal, and that promise means something to me — it means someone in power will not erase my family's belonging while we live here. Yes, TMC is corrupt, Shahjahan took what he wanted, the teacher jobs were sold for bribes — I know all of this, and it is terrible and wrong. But when I stand in this booth, I think of my great-grandfather's name in our land records, and I think of Modi's temples being broadcast to the whole country, and I think of CAA Rules being celebrated by Shantanu for the Matuas while my family watches from outside the line they are drawing. I will not give my vote to a party that questions my documents while telling the nation I do not belong here. TMC will steal, yes, but at least I will still be here, still be Indian, still be safe in my own village.
primary_drivers: BJP's existential threat to Muslim belonging through CAA, Hindu nationalism, and document questioning
Mamata's explicit anti-NRC promise and implicit protection of Muslim voters
Preference for manageable corruption over existential erasure — 'known theft' over 'being erased'
